ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Corn Hoon Kueh; mapped to steamed cake food product as the closest defensible class. Corn Hoon Kueh is a Singaporean/Malay steamed kueh made with green pea flour and coconut milk.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Corn Hoon Kueh. A0CKP (steamed starch-based cake) is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for this green pea flour and coconut milk steamed kueh.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this traditional Singaporean steamed kueh product.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Corn Hoon Kueh is one piece weighing approximately 60 g, commonly consumed as a morning snack or light breakfast item at Singaporean hawker centres and markets.

nutrient_source.title: Corn Hoon Kueh — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Corn Hoon Kueh (cr_id F11090006). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat estimated at ~3.2 g/100 g based on coconut milk contribution consistent with HPB compositional data for similar coconut milk-based kueh; sugar estimated at ~5 g/100 g as HPB record does not separately report sugar. Detailed fatty acid fractions (MUFA, PUFA, trans fat), potassium, cholesterol, vitamins, and water content are not reported in the HPB source for this item and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Green Pea Flour-Based Steamed Cake — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for green pea flour products and steamed starch-based cakes
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Corn Hoon Kueh exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 52 (low) is estimated based on analogous green pea flour-based steamed products and similar coconut milk kueh entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 10.68) / 100 ≈ 5.6, using a 60 g serving providing 10.68 g available carbohydrate (17.8 g/100 g × 0.60). The high dietary fibre content (4.5 g/100 g) supports a lower glycaemic response consistent with this estimate.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (56 mg/100 g) with meaningful dietary fibre (4.5 g/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Corn Hoon Kueh is suitable for individuals with hypertension due to its very low sodium content (56 mg/100 g); however, the saturated fat from coconut milk (~3.2 g/100 g) should be considered, and portion size should be limited to 1–2 pieces per serving to manage overall saturated fat intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (52), moderate carbohydrate (17.8 g/100 g), and high dietary fibre (4.5 g/100 g) moderating glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: Corn Hoon Kueh has a low glycemic index (estimated 52) and a meaningful dietary fibre content of 4.5 g/100 g that slows glucose absorption, making it a manageable snack for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when limited to 1–2 pieces per occasion; individuals should confirm sugar content on packaging as formulations vary.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk (~3.2 g/100 g) is the primary cardiovascular concern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While Corn Hoon Kueh is low in sodium and calories, the saturated fat contributed by coconut milk (~3.2 g/100 g) is a concern for cardiovascular disease management; individuals with CVD should limit consumption to occasional small servings and monitor total daily saturated fat intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk is pro-inflammatory; no meaningful anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Corn Hoon Kueh is not recommended for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis as the primary fat source is coconut milk, which contributes saturated fat (~3.2 g/100 g) that does not qualify for the MUFA/PUFA anti-inflammatory carve-out; individuals with RA should limit or avoid this item.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Corn Hoon Kueh is a traditional Singaporean and Malay steamed kueh (cake) made primarily from green pea flour (hoon kueh flour) and coconut milk, with corn as a flavouring and textural ingredient. The kueh is prepared by mixing green pea flour with coconut milk, sugar, water, and corn kernels, then steaming the mixture until it sets into a soft, slightly firm, translucent or pale yellow cake with a smooth, jelly-like texture and a mildly sweet, coconut-flavoured taste. It is commonly sold at Singaporean hawker centres, wet markets, and Malay kueh stalls, typically as a breakfast or mid-morning snack item. Nutritionally, Corn Hoon Kueh provides 110 kcal per 100 g, with moderate carbohydrate (17.8 g/100 g), low protein (0.9 g/100 g), and moderate fat (3.9 g/100 g) primarily from coconut milk. It is notably high in dietary fibre (4.5 g/100 g) relative to its calorie content, which supports satiety and moderates glycaemic response. Sodium is very low at 56 mg/100 g. The estimated glycemic index is 52 (low), making it a relatively glycaemia-friendly snack when consumed in small portions. The saturated fat content (~3.2 g/100 g from coconut milk) is the primary clinical concern for cardiovascular disease and rheumatoid arthritis management. Corn Hoon Kueh is gluten-free, dairy-free, vegan, and halal, making it suitable for a wide range of dietary requirements. It is classified as a minimally processed traditional snack food.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11090006), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean food items. The match is exact. Saturated fat and sugar are estimated from compositional knowledge of coconut milk-based kueh consistent with HPB data for similar products. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database as no direct measurement exists for this specific item.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available from HPB source): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. Reason: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this traditional Singaporean steamed kueh are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F11090006) as of April 2026. Saturated_fat_g (3.2 g/100 g) is estimated from the known coconut milk contribution in similar HPB-documented coconut milk kueh products. Sugar_g (5.0 g/100 g) is estimated as HPB does not separately report sugar for this item; some commercial versions may contain more added sugar — confirm on packaging. GI value of 52 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on green pea flour-based steamed products; no direct GI measurement for Corn Hoon Kueh was found. Glycemic load per serving calculated at 60 g serving size: (52 × 10.68) / 100 = 5.6. All core macros (protein_g 0.9, fat_g 3.9, carbohydrate_g 17.8) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification required.
